sections.sourcing: No new sourcing-geography shifts this week. Bangladesh's BGMEA hosted an interactive dialogue with SME member factories on 6 July to discuss pathways for improving competitiveness, part of the ongoing industry push (which Primark has previously engaged with) to capture more diversified, higher-value orders from major buyers ahead of Bangladesh's 2026 LDC graduation.
sections.strategy: Primark's two previously announced US stores are now imminent: Willowbrook Mall in Houston, TX opens 16 July and Castleton Square Mall in Indianapolis, IN (Primark's first Indiana store) opens 23 July, taking the US estate to 44 locations. The ABF/Primark demerger remains on track for completion before end of 2027, and incoming CFO Lucy Slinger is set to join in September, continuing the leadership transition flagged in the July trading update.
soWhat: Two US store openings landing within days of each other (Houston 16 July, Indianapolis 23 July) means initial stocking orders for US-bound ranges should already be moving through the supply chain — suppliers on those SKUs should confirm on-time replenishment capacity for the back-to-school push.

BGMEA's continued SME competitiveness dialogue signals Bangladesh is actively courting more diversified, higher-value orders from buyers like Primark ahead of LDC graduation — smaller and mid-tier Bangladeshi suppliers may see increased inquiry activity in H2 2026.

With CFO Lucy Slinger starting in September amid active demerger preparation, expect finance-led scrutiny of supplier costs and payment terms to sharpen later this year as Primark firms up its standalone cost base.
